summary: Full backend (23 pytest cases) + frontend happy-path testing for Threadline SaaS ERP. All 23 backend tests passed (catalog, auth, guest checkout, stock deduction, admin CRUD, POS, coupon apply, low-stock flag, order status update). Frontend home, shop (6 products), product detail (image + size/color selectors + stock indicator), add-to-cart drawer, /login Google sign-in button, /admin unauth redirect to /login, and /admin authenticated (via session_token cookie) rendering Control Room dashboard all verified.

minor.issue: Returns HTTP 404 with body {"detail":"Invalid coupon"} even for existing-but-inactive coupons. Consider 400 for 'inactive/expired' vs 404 for 'not found' for clearer client handling (current test expectation met).
N+1 query pattern (inner selects per row in Python loop). Works fine for small seed but will slow as data grows; recommend eager-loading with joinedload/selectinload.

issues: Recharts console warning 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' on initial paint – harmless flicker but should set explicit min-height on ResponsiveContainer parent.
Radix Dialog warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for DialogContent'. Add <DialogDescription> or aria-describedby for a11y.
document.title still shows default 'Emergent | Fullstack App' — update to 'Threadline' branding.

action_items: Optional: Add DialogDescription/aria-describedby to cart drawer DialogContent to remove Radix a11y warning.
Optional: Give Recharts ResponsiveContainer an explicit height (e.g., h-64) on first render to eliminate width/height=-1 warning.
Optional perf: replace N+1 lookups in admin listing endpoints with SQLAlchemy selectinload joins.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 1250+ lines — should be split into routers (catalog, admin_catalog, orders, hr, marketing) for maintainability (>700 line threshold).

product_to_dict branches on include_details in an unusual way: when include_details=False it still emits full image list in the first branch — the ternary with '... or ([...] if imgs else [])' is unreachable in list view because the first comprehension is always truthy when imgs exist. Logic works but is confusing; simplify.

checkout inventory decrement + stock movement is not in a single atomic transaction lock — concurrent checkouts of the same variant can oversell. Consider SELECT ... FOR UPDATE or quantity check via UPDATE ... WHERE quantity >= qty.
auth.py uses legacy @app.on_event('startup'); fine but deprecated in FastAPI. Move to lifespan context.
Session cookie created in /api/auth/session uses secure=True samesite=none which requires HTTPS (ok for preview), but the Authorization Bearer header path is the only one exercised by tests.

CORS middleware added AFTER app.include_router and with allow_credentials=True + allow_origins=['*'] — browsers reject wildcard with credentials. On preview it still works because the deployment proxy strips CORS; harden for production by setting explicit origins.
test_credentials: Seeded directly in Postgres: user_id='user_testadmin123', email='test.admin@example.com', role='super_admin'; session_token='test_session_abc' (valid 7 days). Usable via Authorization: Bearer test_session_abc OR cookie session_token=test_session_abc.
seed_data_creation: Inserted test super_admin user + 7-day session in Supabase Postgres (users + user_sessions tables). Test product/category/coupon were created and cleaned up within the test suite. One guest order + one coupon-discount order + one POS cash order remain in DB from the run (expected).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Admin test creds persist: Authorization: Bearer test_session_abc (user_testadmin123 / super_admin). If tests left residual orders/customers/notifications, that is intentional for dashboard metrics. Product slugs are dynamic (raw-cut-premium-ce03 etc.) — never hardcode 'merch-hub-54'; always fetch from /api/products first. Stock of the first-matched variant (>=5) gets reduced by ~3 per run (one guest, one coupon, one POS). Re-seed if inventory hits 0.
rca of the issue: No bugs requiring RCA. All requested flows working: public catalog, guest checkout + stock deduction + sale movement logging, coupon percent discount, POS cash order with source=pos & shipping=0, low-stock threshold flag in dashboard/inventory, admin CRUD (categories/products/variants/stock/orders/coupons), role-based 401/403, and frontend render + cookie-based admin auth.
